product.name: ZORYVE (roflumilast) cream 0.15%
product.used_for: Topical treatment of inflammation, redness, and itch associated with atopic dermatitis in adult and pediatric patients 6 years of age and older.

recommendation: ZORYVE cream is a generally safe and effective prescription option for treating mild-to-moderate atopic dermatitis in individuals with Black skin. It offers a non-steroidal alternative that avoids pigment changes, which are a significant concern for people with darker skin tones. However, caution is advised for individuals who are sensitive to parabens or who have acne-prone skin, as some ingredients could cause irritation or clogged pores in susceptible users.

Given only 20% of the long-term data was based on Black patients, we recommend following label instructions closely and consulting a healthcare provider if any irritation, worsening of symptoms, or lack of improvement occurs. For those who wish to avoid parabens entirely, discussing alternative treatments with a dermatologist may be advisable.
active_ingredients: Roflumilast, PDE4 inhibitor. 1.5 mg per gram of cream.
inactive_ingredients: Ceteareth-10 phosphate, cetearyl phosphate, cetostearyl alcohol, diethylene glycol monoethyl ether, hexylene glycol, isopropyl palmitate, methylparaben, propylparaben, purified water, sodium hydroxide, white petrolatum, and hydrochloric acid (may be added to adjust pH).
formulation_concerns: ZORYVE cream contains parabens (methylparaben and propylparaben), which help preserve the product but may be a concern for people who prefer to avoid certain chemicals, even though they are allowed and safe in small amounts.
Isopropyl palmitate may clog pores in people who have acne-prone skin.
Some ingredients like ceteareth-10 phosphate could cause mild irritation in individuals with very sensitive skin, although this is rare.
People with moderate to severe liver disease should not use this product because of how the active ingredient is processed in the body.
Overall, ZORYVE is considered safe for most people when used as directed, but it's wise to watch for any signs of irritation or reactions, especially during the first few uses.
research_analysis: Roflumilast is the main ingredient and helps reduce skin inflammation and itching in eczema.

Fit for Black skin: study programs that included Black/African American participants and darker skin tones show meaningful improvement with once-daily use, with no treatment-related lightening or darkening reported.
Systemic absorption is low, so the risk of effects inside the body is minimal for most users.
Parabens are used as preservatives but have moderate safety concerns for some users who prefer to avoid them.
Overall, most people tolerate the cream well when it's used as prescribed, but users with sensitive skin should monitor for any irritation.
